sidebar.title: Navigation
sidebar.readme_text: This dashboard does not change the model, the pages, the charts, or the visual system. It adds a bilingual presentation layer and clearer labels while preserving the underlying quantitative terminology.
sidebar.brand_subtitle: A clearer, shareable, and still fully quantitative interface for the 2026 World Cup simulation project.
sidebar.data_ready_text: This interface uses the project's default local outputs. It keeps the quantitative content intact while hiding technical file naming from the main navigation.

overview.label: Overview
overview.description: Aggregated tournament surface. Same model. Same charts. Clearer wording only.

groups.label: Groups
groups.description: Group-stage position distributions and group structure without changing the underlying outputs.

stages.label: Tournament states
stages.description: Stage-by-stage survival structure from group exit to champion probability.

pairwise.label: Pairwise matrix
pairwise.description: Full 48×48 pairwise surface from the existing pairwise engine.

team.label: Team view
team.description: Single-team reading panel for final probabilities and structural ratings.

audit.label: Audit & methodology
audit.description: Run audit, aggregated tables, and descriptive statistics.

market.label: Model vs market
market.description: Compare simulated title probabilities to normalized market-implied probabilities without changing the model.

publication.label: Publication layer
publication.description: Public-facing project framing, methodology language, and LinkedIn-ready narrative without changing charts or calculations.

overview.visible_teams: Teams shown in the champion chart
overview.top_third_paths_help: This chart shows the most frequent combinations of groups supplying the best third-placed teams.
overview.top_third_paths_axis: Groups supplying the best third-placed teams
overview.group_strength_help: Mean strength, strongest team level, and spread inside each group.
overview.group_strength_chart: Mean and peak strength by group

groups.best_thirds_help: This chart shows the simulated frequency of the best-third group combinations that feed the knockout bracket. It is normalized defensively if the saved probability field is missing or null.

stages.frontier_slider: Teams shown in the tournament-state comparison

bullets.no_raw_front: No raw billion-line simulation output is injected into the frontend.
bullets.graph_source: Charts are built from run exports and structural team data.
bullets.pairwise_complete: The full pairwise layer contains 1,128 unique matchups.

group_survival_prob.label: Group survival probability
group_survival_prob.help: Probability of reaching the knockout phase from the group stage.

r16_prob.label: Round of 16 probability
r16_prob.help: Probability of reaching the Round of 16.

third_place_prob.label: Third-place probability
third_place_prob.help: Probability of winning the third-place match.

fourth_place_prob.label: Fourth-place probability
fourth_place_prob.help: Probability of losing the third-place match.

fragility.label: Fragility
fragility.help: How strongly tournament odds deteriorate under adverse scenarios.

upside.label: Upside
upside.help: How strongly tournament odds rise under favorable scenarios.

path_dependence.label: Path dependence
path_dependence.help: How strongly tournament odds change depending on the route through the bracket.

group_win_a.label: Group win probability — team A
group_win_a.help: Probability that team A wins in 90 minutes in the group-stage model.

group_draw.label: Group draw probability
group_draw.help: Probability of a draw in 90 minutes in the group-stage model.

group_win_b.label: Group win probability — team B
group_win_b.help: Probability that team B wins in 90 minutes in the group-stage model.

ko_win90_a.label: Knockout win in 90 minutes — team A
ko_win90_a.help: Probability that team A wins in regulation time in the knockout model.

ko_draw90.label: Knockout draw after 90 minutes
ko_draw90.help: Probability that the knockout match is level after 90 minutes.

ko_win90_b.label: Knockout win in 90 minutes — team B
ko_win90_b.help: Probability that team B wins in regulation time in the knockout model.

ko_advance_a.label: Knockout advance probability — team A
ko_advance_a.help: Probability that team A advances after regulation, extra time, or penalties.

ko_advance_b.label: Knockout advance probability — team B
ko_advance_b.help: Probability that team B advances after regulation, extra time, or penalties.

ko_et_a.label: Extra-time survival — team A
ko_et_a.help: Conditional probability contribution from extra time for team A.

ko_et_b.label: Extra-time survival — team B
ko_et_b.help: Conditional probability contribution from extra time for team B.

ko_pen_a.label: Penalty shootout survival — team A
ko_pen_a.help: Conditional probability contribution from penalties for team A.

ko_pen_b.label: Penalty shootout survival — team B
ko_pen_b.help: Conditional probability contribution from penalties for team B.

avg_goals_a.label: Average goals — team A
avg_goals_a.help: Average goals scored by team A in the pairwise Monte Carlo layer.

avg_goals_b.label: Average goals — team B
avg_goals_b.help: Average goals scored by team B in the pairwise Monte Carlo layer.

avg_conduct_a.label: Average conduct score — team A
avg_conduct_a.help: Average fair-play or conduct proxy generated for team A.

avg_conduct_b.label: Average conduct score — team B
avg_conduct_b.help: Average fair-play or conduct proxy generated for team B.

market.no_file: No market odds file found. The page stays available but needs a CSV with columns team,title_american.
market.caption: This page compares model title probabilities to normalized market-implied probabilities. It is not a betting module and does not produce wagering instructions.
market.topn: Teams shown in the model-versus-market comparison

publication.project_summary_text: This local dashboard presents a fully reproducible World Cup 2026 simulation workbench. Current headline result: **{team}** is the model leader at **{prob}** title probability in the active run.
publication.linkedin_text: Built a local World Cup 2026 quantitative simulation dashboard in Python + Streamlit. It combines pairwise Monte Carlo, full tournament propagation, best-third qualification logic, bilingual UI, and a model-vs-market disagreement layer. Current active run leader: {team} at {prob} title probability.
publication.limitations_text: The dashboard does not change the underlying model. It surfaces aggregated results more clearly, preserves technical terminology, and adds a public-facing framing layer. Monte Carlo outputs remain estimates, not certainties.
